# LiveSpec - Information Architecture for AI Agents

LiveSpec is an information architecture and context generation system. Specifications are organized into a structured hierarchy (PURPOSE → requirements → strategy → behaviors) that compiles into AGENTS.md—optimized context for AI agent consumption. Using a five-phase approach (DEFINE → DESIGN → BUILD → VERIFY → EVOLVE), it enables specification-driven regeneration where code is disposable and specs are durable. Built on MSL (Markdown Specification Language), LiveSpec works with any AI coding agent.

**The folder structure is a means. The generated context tree (AGENTS.md) is the end.**

## Core Concepts

**Value Structure:** PURPOSE → foundation/ → strategy/ → features/ (intent flows down, implementations trace up)

**Context Tree:** Workspace specs (HOW you build, applies to all) vs Product specs (WHAT you build, project-specific)

**Three Layers:** Requirements (business-driven) → Strategy (architecture-driven) → Behaviors (implementation-driven)

## Core Methodology (Read in Order)

1. [Phase 0: DEFINE](prompts/0-define/) - Problem definition and workspace setup
   - 0a-quick-start.md - Zero-question setup (5 min, sensible defaults)
   - 0b-customize-workspace.md - Full workspace customization (20-30 min)
   - 0c-define-problem.md - Document project purpose
   - 0d-assess-complexity.md - Evaluate project complexity
   - 0e-evaluate-research-needs.md - Determine if UX research needed
   - 0f-identify-constraints.md - Hard boundaries
   - 0g-define-outcomes.md - Define high-level requirements

2. [Phase 1: DESIGN](prompts/1-design/) - Solution architecture
   - 1a-document-ux-flows.md - UX interaction flows (optional)
   - 1b-design-architecture.md - System structure
   - 1c-define-behaviors.md - Observable behaviors
   - 1d-create-contracts.md - API/data contracts

3. [Phase 2: BUILD](prompts/2-build/) - Implementation
   - 2a-implement-from-specs.md - Build to specifications
   - 2b-create-tests.md - Validate specifications

4. [Phase 3: VERIFY](prompts/3-verify/) - Validation
   - 3a-run-validation.md - Comprehensive validation
   - 3b-acceptance-review.md - Stakeholder approval

5. [Phase 4: EVOLVE](prompts/4-evolve/) - Continuous synchronization
   - 4a-detect-drift.md - Find spec/code misalignment
   - 4b-extract-specs.md - Document new behaviors
   - 4c-sync-complete.md - Confirm synchronization
   - 4d-regenerate-context.md - Update AGENTS.md

## Project Structure

LiveSpec separates workspace (HOW you build) from product (WHAT you build):

```
your-project/
├── PURPOSE.md              # Why this project exists
├── AGENTS.md               # AI agent context (generated)
├── .livespec/              # Methodology (via submodule or copy)
│   ├── prompts/            # Phase prompts
│   │   ├── 0-define/
│   │   ├── 1-design/
│   │   ├── 2-build/
│   │   ├── 3-verify/
│   │   ├── 4-evolve/
│   │   └── utils/
│   ├── templates/          # Spec and config templates
│   ├── standard/           # MSL metaspecs and conventions
│   ├── scripts/            # Validation tools
│   └── guides/             # Deep-dive documentation
└── specs/
    ├── workspace/          # Operating context (how agents/humans/workspace interact)
    │   ├── constitution.spec.md
    │   ├── patterns.spec.md
    │   ├── workflows.spec.md
    │   └── taxonomy.spec.md
    ├── foundation/     # WHY - Strategic and functional requirements
    │   ├── strategic/
    │   └── functional/
    ├── strategy/         # HOW - Architectural approach
    ├── features/        # WHAT - Observable behaviors
    └── interfaces/        # WHAT - API/data contracts
```

## Examples

Working examples demonstrating three-layer architecture:

- [Loan System](examples/loan-system/) - Financial calculations with many-to-many traceability
  - Shows cascade impact analysis when requirements change
  - Demonstrates requirement → strategy → behavior flow

- [E-commerce Checkout](examples/ecommerce-checkout/) - UX-driven specifications
  - Shows research-to-spec flow
  - Demonstrates user personas and flow documentation

- [Auth System](examples/auth-system/) - Security/cross-cutting concerns
  - Authentication and authorization patterns
  - Role-based access control specifications

## Quick Start

### New Project (Recommended: Helper Script)
```bash
# Clone LiveSpec
git clone https://github.com/chrs-myrs/livespec.git

# Run installation script (auto-detects best method)
cd your-project
bash ../livespec/dist/scripts/install-livespec.sh

# Start Phase 0
claude-code "Use .livespec/prompts/0-define/0a-quick-start.md"
```

### Existing Project
```bash
# Install framework
bash /path/to/livespec/dist/scripts/install-livespec.sh

# Extract specs from existing code
claude-code "Use .livespec/prompts/4-evolve/4b-extract-specs.md"
```

### Upgrade Existing Installation
```bash
# For submodule installations
git submodule update --remote .livespec-repo

# For copy installations - reinstall
bash /path/to/livespec/dist/scripts/install-livespec.sh
```

## Code-First Approach (Existing Projects)

For brownfield projects with existing code, start with Phase 4 (EVOLVE):

1. **Extract Specs**: Use [4b-extract-specs.md](prompts/4-evolve/4b-extract-specs.md)
   - Analyzes code to generate specification drafts
   - Marks extractions with `confidence: LOW` and `requires_validation: true`

2. **Validate**: Use [4e-validate-extractions.md](prompts/4-evolve/4e-validate-extractions.md)
   - Review extracted specs against actual implementation
   - Promote LOW → VALIDATED confidence

3. **Maintain Sync**: Use [4a-detect-drift.md](prompts/4-evolve/4a-detect-drift.md)
   - Weekly drift checks identify spec-code misalignment
   - Three drift types: behavioral, contractual, requirements

## MSL Format Example

All LiveSpec specifications use [MSL (Markdown Specification Language)](https://github.com/chrs-myrs/msl-specification):

```markdown
---
criticality: CRITICAL
failure_mode: System unsecured without authentication
---

# User Authentication

## Requirements
- [!] System authenticates users via email and password before allowing access.
  - Valid credentials allow login
  - Invalid credentials rejected
  - Sessions persist after authentication
  - Protected resources require authentication
```

Title, frontmatter, Requirements. Minimal. Testable.

## Key Features

- **Regeneration Flow**: Specs → Code (generate), Code → Specs (level up discoveries)
- **AI Agent Agnostic**: Works with Claude, Copilot, Cursor, any agent
- **Phase 4 Drift Detection**: Continuous synchronization prevents staleness
- **MSL Minimalism**: 70% smaller specifications stay maintainable
- **Workspace Separation**: HOW vs WHAT keeps concerns separated
- **No Lock-in**: Just markdown files and folders

## When Requirements Change

Use cascade impact analysis to find affected specs:

```bash
# Find implementations affected by requirement change
grep -r "satisfies:.*changed-requirement.spec.md" specs/features/
```

**Decision framework:**
- Spec reflects intended behavior → Update code
- Code reflects actual needed behavior → Update spec
- Both unclear → Run validation, update whichever fails

See [loan-system example](examples/loan-system/) for detailed cascade analysis workflow.

## Cross-Cutting Concerns

For concerns spanning multiple modules (auth, logging, error handling):

- **Behavior spec**: WHAT it does (e.g., "System authenticates users via OAuth")
- **Strategy spec**: HOW approach applies broadly (e.g., `strategy/security-approach.spec.md`)
- **Contract spec**: Interface definition (e.g., `interfaces/auth-api.spec.md`)

See [behavior-contract-boundary.md](guides/behavior-contract-boundary.md) and [auth-system example](examples/auth-system/).

## Documentation

Complete guides in [docs/](docs/):
- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md) - Get started in 5 minutes
- [Methodology](docs/methodology.md) - Philosophy and approach
- [MSL Guide](docs/msl-guide.md) - Specification format reference

## Workspace Specifications

See [specs/workspace/](specs/workspace/) for how LiveSpec itself is built:
- [constitution.spec.md](specs/workspace/constitution.spec.md) - Development principles
- [patterns.spec.md](specs/workspace/patterns.spec.md) - Code and spec patterns
- [workflows.spec.md](specs/workspace/workflows.spec.md) - Development workflows

## Prompt Specifications

See [specs/artifacts/prompts/](specs/artifacts/prompts/) for what each prompt accomplishes:
- Each prompt has corresponding .spec.md defining expected behavior
- Specifications use YAML frontmatter linking for traceability
- All prompts validated against their specifications

## Topics

Topics: livespec, living-specifications, context-generation, agents-md, spec-sync, ai-development, msl, minimal-specification-language, regeneration, disposable-code, specifications, requirements, documentation, ai-coding, software-architecture, development-methodology, workspace-specs

## Using with AI Agents

### Claude Code
```bash
claude-code "Use .livespec/prompts/0-define/0a-quick-start.md"
```

### Cursor
Open `.livespec/prompts/0-define/0a-quick-start.md` and use Composer

### GitHub Copilot
Add `.livespec/prompts/0-define/0a-quick-start.md` as context

### Any Agent
Copy/paste prompts - they're plain markdown

### Context7
```bash
# AI agent fetches methodology remotely
"Use @context7/chrs-myrs/livespec methodology to set up this project"
```

## Version and Repository

Version: 4.0.0
Updated: 2025-12-21
Repository: https://github.com/chrs-myrs/livespec
License: MIT

---

*LiveSpec v4.0.0 - Information architecture where code is disposable and specs are durable. Simple. Minimal. Effective.*
